channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Cruise ship waiting for help after 3 people died in a suspected hantavirus outbreak
item.description: A cruise ship with nearly 150 people aboard was waiting for help off the coast of Cape Verde in the Atlantic Ocean after three passengers died in a suspected outbreak of the rare hantavirus.
item.content:encoded:
A cruise ship with nearly 150 people aboard was waiting for help off the coast of Cape Verde in the Atlantic Ocean after three passengers died in a suspected outbreak of the rare hantavirus.

(Image credit: Arilson Almeida)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Russia declares a truce in Ukraine to mark Victory Day
item.description: Russia declared a unilateral ceasefire in Ukraine for Friday and Saturday to mark the defeat of Nazi Germany in World War II, but threatened to strike back at Kyiv if it tries to disrupt festivities.
item.content:encoded:
Russia declared a unilateral ceasefire in Ukraine for Friday and Saturday to mark the defeat of Nazi Germany in World War II, but threatened to strike back at Kyiv if it tries to disrupt festivities.

(Image credit: Mikhail Metzel)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Why U.S. troops are stationed in Germany, and what happens if some leave
item.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with Jeff Rathke, president of the American-German Institute, about U.S. troops stationed in Germany, and what happens if President Trump moves some of them elsewhere.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with Jeff Rathke, president of the American-German Institute, about U.S. troops stationed in Germany, and what happens if President Trump moves some of them elsewhere.
item.dc:creator: Fiona Geiran

item.title: Modi's party takes control of India's West Bengal in key state election
item.description: The outcome is expected to strengthen Modi's political position midway through his third term.
item.content:encoded:
The outcome is expected to strengthen Modi's political position midway through his third term.

(Image credit: Manish Swarup)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The U.S. fights to reopen the Strait of Hormuz as UAE says it's attacked by Iran
item.description: The U.S. military said it battled Iranian forces and sank six small boats as it moved to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, while the United Arab Emirates said Iran fired missiles and drones its way.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. military said it battled Iranian forces and sank six small boats as it moved to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, while the United Arab Emirates said Iran fired missiles and drones its way.

(Image credit: Amirhosein Khorgooi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump says U.S. will 'guide' stranded ships through the Strait. And, key Senate races to watch
item.description: President Trump announced that the U.S will "guide" stranded ships through the Strait of Hormuz. And, Democrats face an uphill battle to gain Senate control. Here are the key races to watch.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump announced that the U.S will "guide" stranded ships through the Strait of Hormuz. And, Democrats face an uphill battle to gain Senate control. Here are the key races to watch.

(Image credit: Roberto Schmidt)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Rescuers help Timmy, a stranded whale, return home
item.description: Rescuers defy warnings as the stranded "Humpback Hope," also known in German media as Timmy, is carried by barge into the North Sea, racing against time to save her.
item.content:encoded: Rescuers defy warnings as the stranded "Humpback Hope," also known in German media as Timmy, is carried by barge into the North Sea, racing against time to save her.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: 2 U.S. service members missing after military exercises in Morocco
item.description: Two U.S. service members are missing in southwestern Morocco after taking part in annual multinational military exercises in the North African country, the United States Africa Command (AFRICOM) said.
item.content:encoded:
Two U.S. service members are missing in southwestern Morocco after taking part in annual multinational military exercises in the North African country, the United States Africa Command (AFRICOM) said.

(Image credit: Mosa'ab Elshamy)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump says the U.S. will 'guide' stranded ships from the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The United States will launch an effort on Monday to "guide" stranded ships from the Iran-gripped Strait of Hormuz, President Donald Trump said, as two ships around the strait reported attacks.
item.content:encoded:
The United States will launch an effort on Monday to "guide" stranded ships from the Iran-gripped Strait of Hormuz, President Donald Trump said, as two ships around the strait reported attacks.

(Image credit: Razieh Poudat/ISNA)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Hantavirus outbreak kills 3 on cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean, WHO says
item.description: Hantaviruses are usually spread by exposure to urine, saliva or feces from infected rodents, such as rats or mice. Hantavirus infections are rare but can cause deadly respiratory infections.
item.content:encoded:
Hantaviruses are usually spread by exposure to urine, saliva or feces from infected rodents, such as rats or mice. Hantavirus infections are rare but can cause deadly respiratory infections.

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein
